name: Eurotrend Inc Which Will Do
recent_news: Eurotrend Inc is a high-end Italian/European furniture company (chairs, sofas, office, hospitality contract). Real address is Tarzana, CA (6004 Reseda Blvd) / NY showroom at 275 Broome St; the 2211 San Ysidro Dr Beverly Hills address in source data appears to be a residential agent address, not an operating site. NOT a school bus or student transportation operator.
fit_notes: NOT A FIT. Misclassified in source list: this is a furniture importer/dealer, not a student-transportation contractor. Exclude from Summit tuck-in funnel.

name: M & J Bus, Inc.
recent_news: Beebes are second-generation school bus operators (Michael started at family's Beebe School Transportation in the 1950s; previously sold prior bus companies in the 1980s to larger firms). Founded M&J in 1995 by buying Robert E. Fellage Inc. and Farmington contract; now ~24+ CT town contracts and reportedly ~600 employees (50 FT per source data). Son MJ active; other son Jason runs separate family auto-recycling business.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate. Large CT footprint (~24 districts), family-owned, prior history of selling earlier ventures to larger firms suggests owners are open to transactions. Beebes are senior with one son in business — moderate succession lever. Densifies Summit/Durham CT route base.

name: Lake Country Industries, LLC (dba Lake Country Transportation)
recent_news: Locally/independently owned MN school bus operator. Received ~$222.6K PPP loan in Jan 2021 covering ~42 employees. Recently announced move from Loretto/Golden Valley to Wayzata. Markets itself as 'premier school bus operator servicing the Twin Cities and Rochester' for daily routes plus activity/athletic trips.
fit_notes: Plausible bolt-on. Small (~40-50 employees), family-owned, Twin Cities + Rochester footprint complements Summit's MN presence. Limited public visibility — need direct outreach to confirm fleet count, contracts, and owner appetite. Geographic relocation to Wayzata may indicate growth phase rather than exit.

name: R E Wilson & Sons, Inc.
principals: James Wilson (President per source data — likely family co-principal)
districts_or_customers: Anne Arundel County Public Schools (AACPS) — routes for Annapolis, Arundel, Broadneck, Crofton, and South River feeder areas; one of several contractors helping move ~7,251 AACPS students/day across the contractor pool
recent_news: Multi-generational MD operator founded 1951, ~75 years in business. USDOT 804776 active. Independent contractor for AACPS — AACPS contractor pool was caught up in a 2024 driver strike-authorization vote, indicating labor pressure across the county's private operators.
fit_notes: Attractive tuck-in profile: 70+ year family business in fragmented AACPS contractor market, classic succession candidate. Small but stable. Summit could consolidate with other AACPS-area independents. Confirm exact fleet count, contract value, and owner age/succession readiness via outreach.

name: Grande Ronde Transportation Co (operating district of Mid Columbia Bus Company, Inc.)
owner_or_parent: Landmark Student Transportation (parent), majority-owned by CC&L Infrastructure (Connor, Clark & Lunn Infrastructure) since Jan 2022; Flatt family sold MidCo to Landmark in 2015
principals: Austin Tibbetts (Location Manager, Grande Ronde Transportation district)
districts_or_customers: historically Pendleton SD (lost contract to First Student in 2022 after 40-year relationship)
plus ~25 other OR districts and ID districts (Twin Falls, Buhl, Hagerman, Valley) under MidCo umbrella
recent_news: NOT INDEPENDENT. Grande Ronde Transportation is the La Grande/eastern Oregon operating district of Mid Columbia Bus Company (MidCo); Pendleton OR address is MidCo HQ. Flatt family sold MidCo to Canadian Landmark Student Transportation in 2015; CC&L Infrastructure acquired majority stake in Landmark in Jan 2022. MidCo lost flagship Pendleton SD contract to First Student effective July 2022. Pursuing EPA grant for up to 10 electric buses on La Grande routes (Dec 2023).
fit_notes: NOT A TUCK-IN TARGET. Already owned by CC&L Infrastructure via Landmark Student Transportation (Canadian PE-backed strategic). Would be a platform-level transaction, not a family tuck-in. Exclude from Summit's family-owned funnel; could be flagged separately if Summit ever considers larger platform M&A.
